items.prompt: Describe the rigid transformation that maps one figure onto the other on a coordinate grid.
items.expected_evidence: identify named numerals in mixed sets
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates identify named numerals in mixed sets with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Classify the triangle and justify the classification using its defining attributes.
items.expected_evidence: say the number name for shown numerals and select a numeral for a spoken number
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates say the number name for shown numerals and select a numeral for a spoken number with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Find a missing angle or side measure using the structure of the diagram.
items.expected_evidence: write bounded early numerals with stable orientation and distinguishing features
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner demonstrates write bounded early numerals with stable orientation and distinguishing features with mathematically valid structure.

items.prompt: Explain which properties remain unchanged under a rigid transformation and why that supports congruence.
items.expected_evidence: identify named numerals in mixed sets
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to identify named numerals in mixed sets.

items.prompt: Explain why the figure belongs to a category in the shape hierarchy.
items.expected_evidence: say the number name for shown numerals and select a numeral for a spoken number
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the structure coherently and ties the explanation back to say the number name for shown numerals and select a numeral for a spoken number.

items.prompt: A student claims two figures are congruent because they look the same size by eye. Diagnose the justification gap. Focus on symbol name instability.
items.expected_evidence: identify named numerals in mixed sets
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward identify named numerals in mixed sets.

items.prompt: A student reflects a point across the x-axis but changes both coordinates. Identify the transformation error. Focus on orientation reversal.
items.expected_evidence: say the number name for shown numerals and select a numeral for a spoken number
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the dominant breakdown, names the structural issue accurately, and redirects the work toward say the number name for shown numerals and select a numeral for a spoken number.

items.prompt: Use a transformation or coordinate argument to justify whether two figures are congruent in a less-familiar diagram.
items.expected_evidence: identify named numerals in mixed sets
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves identify named numerals in mixed sets in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: Apply angle or triangle reasoning in a contextual or composite-figure setting.
items.expected_evidence: say the number name for shown numerals and select a numeral for a spoken number
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner preserves say the number name for shown numerals and select a numeral for a spoken number in a changed surface form or representation.

items.prompt: After a delay, describe a rigid motion on a grid and explain which measurements are preserved.
items.expected_evidence: identify named numerals in mixed sets
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner still demonstrates identify named numerals in mixed sets on a delayed recheck without a blocking recurrence.

items.prompt: Check cardinality first so the system can distinguish an upstream spatial-structure gap from a true numeral recognition and writing readiness misunderstanding.
items.expected_evidence: shows whether cardinality is secure enough to support numeral recognition and writing readiness
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner response makes it possible to distinguish an upstream gap in cardinality from a current-node issue in numeral recognition and writing readiness.
